title: Multicenter iEEG dataset for classification of graphoelements and artifactual signals (FNUSA)
description: A multicenter intracranial electroencephalography (iEEG) dataset acquired from patients undergoing clinical evaluation at St. Anne's University Hospital in Brno, Czech Republic. The dataset comprises 30-minute awake resting interictal recordings sampled at 5 kHz using standard depth electrodes, with detailed electrode localization and seizure onset zone annotations. This resource is designed to support the development and validation of automated classification methods for graphoelements and artifactual signals in clinical iEEG recordings.
methods_description: iEEG recordings were acquired using standard intracranial depth electrodes (5/10/15-contact semi-flexible, ALCIS, Platinum; 2 mm contact length, 0.8 mm diameter, 1.5 mm inter-contact spacing) at 25 kHz using M&I BrainScope equipment, then downsampled to 5 kHz. Power line frequency: 50 Hz. Recordings consisted of 30-minute awake resting interictal sessions. Electrode contacts were annotated with shaft group, seizure onset zone status, and anatomical location where available.
